steps.instruction: Substitute the result into the outer function $f$.

title: Analysing a piecewise function for continuity
problem: Consider the piecewise function $$f(x) = \begin{cases} x^2 & \text{if } x < 2 \\ 3x - 2 & \text{if } x \geq 2 \end{cases}$$ Evaluate $f(2)$, find the left-hand limit at $x = 2$, and determine whether the function is continuous at $x = 2$.

steps.instruction: Find $f(2)$ by identifying which piece applies when $x = 2$. Since $x = 2$ satisfies $x \geq 2$, use the second piece.
Find the left-hand limit: the value $f(x)$ approaches as $x \to 2$ from the left. Substitute $x = 2$ into the first piece $x^2$.
Since the left-hand limit equals $f(2)$, state the conclusion about continuity.
steps.working: $$\text{The left and right values agree, so } f \text{ is continuous at } x = 2.$$

answer: $f(2) = 4$, $\lim_{x \to 2^-} f(x) = 4$. The function is continuous at $x = 2$.

title: Solving an absolute value equation and finding the vertex
problem: Solve $|2x + 3| = 11$ and find the vertex of the graph $y = |2x + 3|$.

steps.instruction: Write two cases. Since $|\text{expression}| = k$ means the expression equals $k$ or $-k$, set up both equations.
Find the vertex of $y = |2x + 3|$. The vertex occurs where the expression inside the absolute value equals zero. Set $2x + 3 = 0$ and solve.

answer: $x = 4$ or $x = -7$. The vertex of $y = |2x + 3|$ is at $\left(-\dfrac{3}{2},\, 0\right)$.
